concept_name: How Big Is the Circle? — r = mv/qB for a Charge in a Uniform Field
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism — table-of-contents reference only (the 'circular motion of a charge in a uniform field' heading, §26.3 scope); teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only. This concept is the RADIUS / SIZE sibling of magnetic_force_moving_charge: it teaches that a charge moving perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field travels in a CLOSED circle whose radius is r = mv/qB — bigger with more momentum (m or v, the numerator), tighter with more grip (q or B, the denominator). The single surfaced quantity is r, and only as a RELATIVE readout (a yellow bar and 'wider/tighter than before' captions) — never a metres value. It deliberately does NOT teach the period T = 2 pi m / (q B) (that belongs to the future cyclotron_period_independent_of_speed), the force magnitude F = qvB sin(theta) (that belongs to magnetic_force_moving_charge), or any current-loop / Ampere / dipole content; no period, frequency, rev-per-sec, force magnitude, Newton or Tesla number ever reaches the canvas (hide_period_readout + hide_magnitude_readout on every state). Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + straightforward contrast beats). Renders on field_3d via the radius_in_uniform_field scenario (the radius-only sibling of lorentz_force_uniform_field), which reuses the orbit basis + circle math and adds the four-factor R_visual scaling, the dashed radius line, the relative radius readout, and the ghost-compare frozen prior circle. Lighter atom by founder carve-out: no assessment block, no coverage_map. Retrofitted from the Socratic model to the straightforward Rule 31 model (2026-07-04): guided distinct-motion beats (no predict-then-reveal prediction pauses, no wait_for_answer, no pause_after_ms; narration drives on-canvas glow, motion runs on the state clock), each state exposing only its own contextual control (per-state visible_controls — S1/S2/S3 zero-control watch beats, S4 numerator m+v, S5 denominator q+B, S6 explore exposes m/v/q/B), reusing the existing radius_in_uniform_field field_3d scenario (per-state contextual-control panel added the same gated way as the circular_loop cl_ pattern: rad_*_row wrappers + applyVisibleControls + radInteracted trusted-grab seize + on-entry seed from variable_overrides).

real_world_anchor.primary: In a mass spectrometer, a lab tool used to separate the isotopes of an element, atoms are given the same speed and sent into the same magnet. A heavier atom carves out a WIDER circle than a lighter one, so the two land at different spots on the detector. The size of the circle literally reads out the atom's weight: r = mv/qB, so for the same speed, same charge and same field, a bigger mass means a bigger circle. Indian labs separate isotopes for medicine and research exactly this way.
real_world_anchor.secondary: At the VECC cyclotron in Kolkata, charged particles are whipped round and round through a uniform magnetic field. It is the same machine you met when learning that a magnetic force only steers — but now look at the SIZE of the loop. As the particles are sped up, their speed v rises, so r = mv/qB grows and the loop spirals outward into a wider and wider circle, exactly as the radius formula predicts.

m.name: Mass of the moving charge (numerator of r = mv/qB; bigger m = wider circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

v.name: Speed of the moving charge (numerator of r = mv/qB; faster = wider circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

q_mag.name: Magnitude of the charge (denominator of r = mv/qB; more charge = tighter circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

B.name: Magnetic field strength (denominator of r = mv/qB; stronger field = tighter circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

formulas.centripetal_balance: The perpendicular magnetic force IS the centripetal force: q v B = m v^2 / r
formulas.radius: Solve the balance for r: r = m v / (q B) — the only quantity this concept surfaces
formulas.numerator_is_momentum: The top of r = mv/qB is the momentum m times v: more momentum is harder to turn, so the circle is wider
formulas.denominator_is_grip: The bottom of r = mv/qB is the field's grip q times B: more grip turns it harder, so the circle is tighter

constraints: Teaches ONLY the radius r = m v / (q B) — the single surfaced quantity. Slider INPUT values for m, v, q, B are allowed; no other number reaches the canvas.
The radius readout is RELATIVE (wider or tighter than a ghost), never an absolute metres value, never a Newton or Tesla number.
NO period T = 2 pi m / (q B) anywhere — no T, no seconds-per-orbit, no rev-per-sec, no frequency f. Every state flags hide_period_readout: true.
NO force magnitude — no F = q v B, no q v B sin(theta), no Newton number. Every state inherits hide_magnitude_readout: true; the F arrow is a direction-only fixed glyph (Rule 29).
NO Ampere, NO current loop, NO magnetic dipole, NO solenoid.

The magnetic force is perpendicular to v at every instant, so it does no work, the speed |v| stays constant, and the path is a CLOSED circle (the why, inherited from the no-work sibling, not re-derived here).
The force points to ONE fixed centre with constant size, which is uniform circular motion: q v B = m v^2 / r.
Radius = momentum over grip: r grows with m or v (numerator), shrinks with q or B (denominator). A bigger B makes the circle TIGHTER, never bigger (this confronts the M1 misconception).
v makes a right angle with B throughout (perpendicular-entry only); the helical or oblique case is out of scope.

aha_moment.statement: More momentum widens the circle; more grip tightens it — size is mv over qB.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Raising B (then q) shrinks the live circle inside a frozen ghost, proving the denominator tightens the turn while the numerator (m, v) widened it in the state before.

STATE_1.title: It Doesn't Just Curve — It Closes

scene_composition.text: a charge crosses a uniform magnetic field — and its path is already curving

the curve bites its
own tail — the trail
snaps shut
it closes into a perfect circle — how big is that circle?

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge moves through a uniform magnetic field, and its path is already curving.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge एक uniform magnetic field से होकर move करता है, और उसका path पहले से ही curve हो रहा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge ఒక uniform magnetic field గుండా move చేస్తుంది, మరియు దాని path ఇప్పటికే curve అవుతోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the curve bite its own tail — the trail snaps shut.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए, curve अपनी ही tail को काटता है — trail बंद हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి, curve దాని స్వంత tail ని కొరుకుతుంది — trail మూసుకుపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: It closes into a perfect circle, and the whole question now is how big that circle is.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक perfect circle में बंद हो जाता है, और अब पूरा सवाल यह है कि वह circle कितना बड़ा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక perfect circle లోకి మూసుకుపోతుంది, మరియు ఇప్పుడు మొత్తం ప్రశ్న ఏమిటంటే ఆ circle ఎంత పెద్దది.

scene_composition.text: a same-size pull, always to one spot

a radius line from
the centre stays the
SAME length all the
way round
a constant pull to one centre is uniform circular motion

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnetic force stays the same size and always points to one centre while the charge orbits.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetic force का size वही रहता है और charge के orbit करते समय यह हमेशा एक ही centre की ओर point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetic force అదే size లో ఉంటుంది మరియు charge orbit చేస్తున్నప్పుడు ఇది ఎల్లప్పుడూ ఒకే centre వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A radius line drawn from that centre stays exactly the same length the whole way round.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उस centre से खींची गई radius line पूरे रास्ते में बिल्कुल same length की रहती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ centre నుండి గీసిన radius line మొత్తం మార్గంలో సరిగ్గా same length లో ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A constant pull toward one centre is the recipe for uniform circular motion — a perfect circle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक ही centre की ओर constant pull, uniform circular motion की recipe है — एक perfect circle।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒకే centre వైపు constant pull, uniform circular motion కి recipe — ఒక perfect circle.

STATE_3.title: The Magnetic Force IS the Centripetal Force

scene_composition.text: circular motion needs a centre force of size mv-squared over r

the magnetic force
IS the centre force
-> r = mv/qB

that one line is the
whole concept: the
circle's size is mv
over qB

tts_sentences.text_en: Circular motion needs a centre-pointing force, and the magnetic force is the only force acting here.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Circular motion को एक centre की ओर point करने वाली force चाहिए, और यहाँ एकमात्र force magnetic force ही है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Circular motion కి centre వైపు point చేసే force కావాలి, మరియు ఇక్కడ ఉన్న ఏకైక force magnetic force మాత్రమే.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the magnetic force is the centripetal force: q v B equals m v-squared over r.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो magnetic force ही centripetal force है: q v B equals m v-squared over r।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి magnetic force నే centripetal force: q v B equals m v-squared over r.

tts_sentences.text_en: Solve that for the radius and you get r equals m v over q B — that one line is the whole concept.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे radius के लिए solve कीजिए और आपको मिलता है r equals m v over q B — वही एक line पूरा concept है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాన్ని radius కోసం solve చేయండి, మీకు వస్తుంది r equals m v over q B — ఆ ఒక్క line మొత్తం concept.

scene_composition.text: m and v on top — heavier or faster widens the circle

a faint ghost marks
the old circle — the
live one swells past
it, twice
m and v are on top — more momentum mv is harder to turn

tts_sentences.text_en: Mass m and velocity v sit on top of the formula, in the numerator.
tts_sentences.text_hi: mass m और velocity v formula के ऊपर, numerator में बैठते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: mass m మరియు velocity v formula పైన, numerator లో ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Make the charge heavier and the live circle swells outward, past a faint ghost of the old circle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge को heavier बनाइए और live circle बाहर की ओर swells होता है, पुराने circle के एक faint ghost से आगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge ని heavier చేయండి, live circle బయటకి swells అవుతుంది, పాత circle యొక్క ఒక faint ghost ని దాటి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Speed it up and it swells again — more momentum, mass m times velocity v, is simply harder to turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे speed up कीजिए और यह फिर से swells होता है — ज़्यादा momentum, mass m times velocity v, को मोड़ना बस मुश्किल है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దీన్ని speed up చేయండి, ఇది మళ్ళీ swells అవుతుంది — ఎక్కువ momentum, mass m times velocity v, ని తిప్పడం కష్టం.

misconception_watch.belief: A heavier charge makes a tighter circle, because it is harder to move.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When m is raised, the live circle swells OUTWARD past the frozen ghost — wider, not tighter.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: m is on top of r = mv/qB, so more mass means a WIDER circle.

misconception_watch.belief: Speeding the charge up does not change the size of the circle.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When v is raised in the second reveal, the live circle swells past a second ghost, just as raising m did.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: v is on top too, so more speed also makes a WIDER circle.

STATE_5.title: More Charge or Stronger Field -> Tighter Circle

scene_composition.text: q and B on the bottom — more field or charge tightens the circle

inside the ghost now
— a stronger field
grips harder, so the
circle shrinks

q and B are on the
bottom — qB is the
grip that tightens
the turn

tts_sentences.text_en: Charge q and magnetic field B sit on the bottom of the formula, in the denominator.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge q और magnetic field B formula के नीचे, denominator में बैठते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge q మరియు magnetic field B formula కింద, denominator లో ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Turn the magnetic field up and the live circle shrinks inward, tighter, sitting inside the ghost.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetic field को up कीजिए और live circle अंदर की ओर shrinks होता है, tighter, ghost के अंदर बैठकर।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetic field ని up చేయండి, live circle లోపలికి shrinks అవుతుంది, tighter, ghost లోపల ఉండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Add more charge and it tightens again — charge q times magnetic field B is the grip that turns it harder.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और charge add कीजिए और यह फिर से tighten होता है — charge q times magnetic field B वह grip है जो इसे और harder मोड़ती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇంకా charge add చేయండి, ఇది మళ్ళీ tighten అవుతుంది — charge q times magnetic field B అనేది దీన్ని harder గా తిప్పే grip.

misconception_watch.belief: A stronger magnetic field gives a BIGGER circle.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When B is raised, the live circle shrinks INWARD, sitting inside the frozen ghost — tighter, never bigger.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: B is in the denominator of r = mv/qB, so more field means a TIGHTER circle.

misconception_watch.belief: More charge means more force, so the circle should get bigger.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When q is raised in the second reveal, the live circle shrinks again, inside the ghost.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: q is in the denominator too: more charge grips harder and tightens the turn.

scene_composition.text: four sliders — m, v, q, B — and a live radius readout

double m and double q
-> the circle returns
to its old size
top two widen it, bottom two tighten it — the readout follows r = mv/qB

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Four sliders — mass m, velocity v, charge q, magnetic field B — and a live radius readout.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। चार sliders — mass m, velocity v, charge q, magnetic field B — और एक live radius readout।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు. నాలుగు sliders — mass m, velocity v, charge q, magnetic field B — మరియు ఒక live radius readout.

tts_sentences.text_en: The top two widen the circle, the bottom two tighten it, and the readout always follows r equals m v over q B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ऊपर के दो circle को widen करते हैं, नीचे के दो इसे tighten करते हैं, और readout हमेशा r equals m v over q B को follow करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: పైన ఉన్న రెండు circle ని widen చేస్తాయి, కింద ఉన్న రెండు దీన్ని tighten చేస్తాయి, మరియు readout ఎల్లప్పుడూ r equals m v over q B ని follow చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Double the mass m and double the charge q together, and the circle comes right back to its old size.
tts_sentences.text_hi: mass m को double कीजिए और charge q को भी double कीजिए, और circle अपनी old size पर वापस आ जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: mass m ని double చేయండి మరియు charge q ని కూడా double చేయండి, circle దాని old size కి తిరిగి వస్తుంది.

STATE_1.label: It doesn't just curve — it closes

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge moves through a uniform magnetic field, and its path is already curving.
Watch the curve bite its own tail — the trail snaps shut.
It closes into a perfect circle, and the whole question now is how big that circle is.
The magnetic force stays the same size and always points to one centre while the charge orbits.
A radius line drawn from that centre stays exactly the same length the whole way round.
A constant pull toward one centre is the recipe for uniform circular motion — a perfect circle.

STATE_3.label: The magnetic force IS the centripetal force

tts_sentences.text_en: Circular motion needs a centre-pointing force, and the magnetic force is the only force acting here.
So the magnetic force is the centripetal force: q v B equals m v-squared over r.
Solve that for the radius and you get r equals m v over q B — that one line is the whole concept.
Mass m and velocity v sit on top of the formula, in the numerator.
Make the charge heavier and the live circle swells outward, past a faint ghost of the old circle.
Speed it up and it swells again — more momentum, mass m times velocity v, is simply harder to turn.

STATE_5.label: More charge or stronger field -> tighter circle

tts_sentences.text_en: Charge q and magnetic field B sit on the bottom of the formula, in the denominator.
Turn the magnetic field up and the live circle shrinks inward, tighter, sitting inside the ghost.
Add more charge and it tightens again — charge q times magnetic field B is the grip that turns it harder.
Your turn. Four sliders — mass m, velocity v, charge q, magnetic field B — and a live radius readout.
The top two widen the circle, the bottom two tighten it, and the readout always follows r equals m v over q B.
Double the mass m and double the charge q together, and the circle comes right back to its old size.
